overview.introduction: Coordinate features integration across remote servers, resolve conflicts, and run collaborative team branching models.
overview.why_it_matters: Understanding Team Workflows is fundamental to collaborate in any modern engineering organization.
overview.industry_usage: Used daily by millions of developers to isolate features development and deploy software securely.
overview.what_you_will_build: A series of configured local Git repositories tracking file changes, branches, stashes, and commit histories.
learning_outcomes: Explain the primary architecture of Team Workflows.
Exert master-level control over Git CLI utilities.
Inspect files tracking states using status and logs commands.
Diagnose and resolve code merge conflicts.

key_concepts.title: Remote Repos & Syncs
key_concepts.definition: Remote repositories are versions of your project hosted on the internet or network. Fetching updates metadata; pulling merges changes.
why_important: Provides rollback points for production bugs.
key_concepts.explanation: When executing Team Workflows workflows, the index metadata records changed files state before writing permanent commits.

commands.name: git status
commands.purpose: Query modified, untracked, and staged files status.
commands.expected_output:
On branch main
nothing to commit, working tree clean
commands.output_explanation: Indicates that all local file edits have been successfully committed to repository history.

commands.name: git log
commands.expected_output:
a1b2c3d feat: add home button
d4e5f6g fix: align login text
commands.output_explanation: Lists unique commit hash identifiers along with commit logs in reverse chronological order.

key_concepts.pro_tip: Keep commit messages descriptive and write them in the imperative mood (e.g. 'fix: bug' instead of 'fixed bug').
key_concepts.best_practices: Create a .gitignore file immediately to avoid committing temporary build artifacts, configs, and system files.
key_concepts.common_mistakes: Executing hard resets or force pushes on shared branch lines without aligning with team members first.
key_concepts.real_world_usage: CI/CD pipelines trigger automated compilation checks upon receiving git push commits to remote repositories.
key_concepts.summary: Team Workflows form the baseline of secure, modern devops engineering pipelines.

interactive_examples.scenario: Inspect untracked changes inside your directory workspace.
interactive_examples.real_world_use: Auditing commits history tracking before push.
interactive_examples.common_mistakes: Running git commands outside initialized git directory workspaces.
interactive_examples.tips: Add '--short' or '-s' flags to see git status in a compact format.

lessons.title: 1. Initial Check (List remote repositories)
lessons.instruction: List configured remote repository connections with their fetch/push URLs.

lessons.title: 2. Check status
lessons.instruction: Run git status to see modified files.

lessons.title: 3. Audit Log History
lessons.explanation: Displays timeline log of previous commits.
lessons.instruction: Print commit history log to console screen.

lessons.title: 5. Show ref mappings
lessons.explanation: Resolve tags and branches SHA identifiers.
lessons.instruction: Query refs maps inside repository workspace.

lessons.title: 6. Show active branch
lessons.explanation: Queries branch name where checkout pointer HEAD is sitting.
lessons.instruction: List all branches using git branch.

lessons.title: 7. Audit reflog
lessons.explanation: Audits reflog containing history revisions of pointers checkout switches.

lessons.title: Mini Challenge: Resolve Merge Conflict
lessons.definition: No step-by-step instructions are provided here. Execute this task directly.
lessons.explanation: Complete this task on your own.
lessons.instruction: A conflict is active in 'conflict.txt'. Open the file, clean the git conflict markers, stage, and commit the resolution.
lessons.example: echo 'Resolved Content' > conflict.txt && git add conflict.txt && git commit -m 'fix: resolve merge conflict'
lessons.hint: Run command: echo 'Resolved Content' > conflict.txt && git add conflict.txt && git commit -m 'fix: resolve merge conflict'
lessons.solution: echo 'Resolved Content' > conflict.txt && git add conflict.txt && git commit -m 'fix: resolve merge conflict'

exercises.title: Configure Git attributes
exercises.problem: Practice git workspace command configurations by querying history logs.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of Git Team Workflows?
options: To track changes in source code files history.
quiz.answer: To track changes in source code files history.
quiz.explanation: Git tracks historical revisions of files inside source controlled projects.

quiz.question: Which area stores staged changes in Git before they are committed?
quiz.explanation: The staging area, also known as the index, holds edits prepared for the next commit.

quiz.question: Which command initializes a new local Git repository?
quiz.explanation: git init initializes a new empty Git repository in the current directory.

quiz.question: What does 'git add' do?
options: Adds changes in the working directory to the staging area.
Creates a new commit in the repository.
quiz.answer: Adds changes in the working directory to the staging area.
quiz.explanation: git add stages current changes to prepare them for commit.

quiz.question: What is the commit message flag in 'git commit'?
quiz.explanation: The '-m' flag specifies the commit message inline.

quiz.question: Which command lists all commits in reverse chronological order?
quiz.explanation: git log displays the commit history for the active branch.

quiz.question: What is HEAD in Git?
options: A pointer to the currently checked-out commit.
The first commit in the repository.
quiz.answer: A pointer to the currently checked-out commit.
quiz.explanation: HEAD points to the active commit or branch pointer in your local repository.

quiz.question: Which command creates a new branch named 'dev'?
quiz.explanation: git branch [name] creates a new branch pointer at the current commit.

quiz.question: How do you switch to an existing branch named 'dev'?
quiz.explanation: git checkout [branch] updates files in the working directory to match the target branch.

quiz.question: What is a merge conflict in Git?
options: A situation where changes in different branches cannot be merged automatically.
quiz.answer: A situation where changes in different branches cannot be merged automatically.
quiz.explanation: Conflicts occur when different commits modify the same lines in a file, requiring manual resolution.

resources.summary: Learn more about Git Team Workflows through official documentation, guides, and manuals.

links.title: Git Team Workflows Documentation Guide
links.description: Official documentation and concepts manual for Team Workflows.

links.title: Interactive Git Branching Tutorial
links.description: Learn Git branching visually in your browser.

links.title: Git Cheat Sheet
links.description: Quick reference guide for common git control commands.

resources.cheat_sheet:
git status                    # Check repository status
git log --oneline            # Show commit logs
git add <file>               # Stage file
git commit -m <msg>          # Commit changes

commands_table.name: git add
commands_table.description: Add file contents to the staging area index.

revision_notes: Git is distributed; every client has a full copy of the project history.
Commits represent snapshots, not diff changes.
Always check git status before committing.
best_practices: Write clear, concise commit messages in the imperative mood.
Keep commits small and focused on single feature changes.
Configure a global gitignore to prevent cluttering.
beginner_mistakes: Committing sensitive database keys or secrets to public repositories.
Modifying commits history that has already been pushed to public team branches.

interview_questions.question: What is Git Team Workflows?
interview_questions.answer: It represents the source code version control mechanism for Team Workflows in development teams.

books.title: Pro Git — Scott Chacon
books.description: The definitive resource guide explaining Git internals and configurations.
